title: LAMP: Lane-Aligned Motion Primitives for Feasible Trajectory Prediction
abstract: arXiv:2606.26661v1 Announce Type: cross  Abstract: Motion forecasting is essential for autonomous driving systems to enable safe decision-making and planning in complex driving scenarios. While existing predictors excel at minimizing standard displacement errors, they often overlook the adherence to lane topology of multimodal predictions, particularly for lower-probability modes. Consequently, predicted trajectories may violate physical and logical constraints, making the prediction set unreliable for safety-critical planning. In this paper, we propose LAMP (Lane-Aligned Motion Primitives), a topology-aware forecasting framework that anchors multimodal prediction to structured motion primitives aligned with lane topology. Specifically, we use a VQ-VAE to learn shape-aware motion primitives as discrete intention queries, capturing spatiotemporal patterns beyond endpoint-based intentions. We further introduce a feasibility-aware intention selector trained with a lane-topology prior for

Title: LAMP: Lane-Aligned Motion Primitives for Feasible Trajectory Prediction
Abstract: arXiv:2606.26661v1 Announce Type: cross  Abstract: Motion forecasting is essential for autonomous driving systems to enable safe decision-making and planning in complex driving scenarios. While existing predictors excel at minimizing standard displacement errors, they often overlook the adherence to lane topology of multimodal predictions, particularly for lower-probability modes. In this paper, we propose LAMP (Lane-Aligned Motion Primitives), a topology-aware forecasting framework that anchors multimodal prediction to structured motion primitives aligned with lane topology. Specifically, we use a VQ-VAE to learn shape-aware motion primitives as discrete intention queries, capturing spatiotemporal patterns beyond endpoint-based intentions. translated_title: LAMP：用于可行轨迹预测的车道对齐运动基元
translated_abstract: arXiv:2606.26661v1 公告类型：交叉 摘要：运动预测对于自动驾驶系统在复杂驾驶场景中实现安全决策和规划至关重要。虽然现有的预测模型在最小化标准位移误差方面表现出色，但它们常常忽略了多模态预测中与车道拓扑的一致性，尤其是对于低概率模式。因此，预测的轨迹可能违反物理和逻辑约束，使得预测集在安全关键的规划中不可靠。本文提出了LAMP（车道对齐运动基元），一种拓扑感知的预测框架，将多模态预测锚定到与车道拓扑对齐的结构化运动基元上。具体来说，我们使用VQ-VAE来学习形状感知的运动基元作为离散意图查询，捕获超越基于端点的意图的时空模式。我们进一步引入了一个基于车道拓扑先验训练的可行性感知意图选择器。
tldr: 本文提出LAMP框架，通过VQ-VAE学习车道对齐的运动基元作为离散意图查询，并引入可行性感知意图选择器，解决了现有运动预测模型忽略车道拓扑导致轨迹不可行的问题。
en_tdlr: This paper proposes LAMP, a lane-aligned motion primitive framework using VQ-VAE to learn shape-aware intention queries and a feasibility-aware selector, addressing the issue of infeasible trajectories in multimodal prediction by enforcing lane topology adherence.
